subject:
Re: Problems with Bayesian Polishing in Relion from Exported WARP
 motion Data
body:
Random thought: not everyone collects movies as LZW-TIFF... 😅 

The possible generalized solution is to make sure the path to the aligned 
averages is the same between the micrographs.star  and particles.star


On Monday, February 8, 2021 at 11:09:53 AM UTC-8 thomas.la...@gmail.com 
wrote:

> Hi Dylan,
>
> For polishing of Warp pre-processed data, I've had to edit the paths 
> listed for the micrographs in the .star files output by Warp a bit.
> Could be as simple as changing the .tif to .mrc of the _rlnMicrographName 
> name column in the particle.star depending on how the directory structure 
> is set-up.
>
> Depending on where the Relion directory is in relation to the Warp frames 
> directory, the necessary manipulation may vary.
> Out of person preference, I like to create a separate Relion projectory 
> directory outside of frames and then sym-link the Warp frames directory 
> within side it.
>
> Then, using sed, I edit the path for the _rlnMicrographName column of the 
> particle.star file to point to the frames/average/someMicrograph_0001.mrc 
> instead of the .tif version.
> May need to do something similar to the micrographs_list.star to make sure 
> the _rlnMicrographName column is also pointing to the right place.
>
> If the particles.star already references the .mrc instead of the .tif, 
> something more nefarious might be going on and the error output from the 
> Relion polishing job might be diagnostic of the problem.
>
> Hope that helps,
> -Tom
>
> On Monday, February 8, 2021 at 1:41:04 AM UTC-8 Dylan Noone wrote:
>
>> Hello all,
>>
>> I obtained a data set and used WARP for preprocessing on the fly. The 
>> folks over at the institution where we collected the data said they 
>> exported it all so that it would be compatible in Relion. So far it has 
>> been behaving very nicely (e.g. in CTF refinement etc). But for polishing I 
>> seem to be getting into lots of errors. I was wondering if someone could 
>> help me.
>>
>> Basically the output I have from WARP is a .star file with the following 
>> format:
>>
>>  *data_*
>>
>>  
>>
>> *loop_*
>>
>> *_rlnMicrographName #1*
>>
>> *_rlnMagnification #2*
>>
>> *_rlnDetectorPixelSize #3*
>>
>> *_rlnVoltage #4*
>>
>> *_rlnSphericalAberration #5*
>>
>> *_rlnAmplitudeContrast #6*
>>
>> *_rlnPhaseShift #7*
>>
>> *_rlnDefocusU #8*
>>
>> *_rlnDefocusV #9*
>>
>> *_rlnDefocusAngle #10*
>>
>> *_rlnCtfMaxResolution #11*
>>
>> *_rlnBeamTiltX #12*
>>
>> *_rlnBeamTiltY #13*
>>
>> *_rlnMicrographMetadata #14*
>>
>> *  
>> average/FoilHole_12403595_Data_12398682_12398684_20201213_112117_fractions.mrc  
>> 11961.7  1.0  300.0  2.7000  0.100  0.0   7068.5   6364.8    2.0  2.7  
>> 0.00  0.00  
>> motion/FoilHole_12403595_Data_12398682_12398684_20201213_112117_fractions.star……………..*
>>
>> As there is a link to the motion folder I thought this would work for 
>> polishing. However, according to the WARP “manual” (
>> http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=371) there should be a motion folder 
>> with a single .star file with all motion information for all the 
>> micrographs. Indeed in this data set there is a motion folder, but it 
>> contains many star files, one for each micrograph. The format is as follows:
>>
>> data_general
>>
>> _rlnImageSizeX                                                            
>>                          11520
>>
>> _rlnImageSizeY                                                            
>>                           8184
>>
>> _rlnImageSizeZ                                                            
>>                             50
>>
>> _rlnMicrographMovieName          
>>  FoilHole_12403595_Data_12398691_12398693_20201213_112126_fractions.mrc
>>
>> _rlnMicrographBinning                                                    
>>                         2.00000
>>
>> _rlnMicrographOriginalPixelSize                                          
>>                         0.41800
>>
>> _rlnMicrographDoseRate                                                    
>>                        1.00000
>>
>> _rlnMicrographPreExposure                                                
>>                               0
>>
>> _rlnVoltage                                                              
>>                             300
>>
>> _rlnMicrographStartFrame                                                  
>>                              1
>>
>> _rlnMotionModelVersion                                                    
>>                              0
>>
>>
>> data_global_shift
>>
>>
>> loop_
>>
>> _rlnMicrographFrameNumber #1
>>
>> _rlnMicrographShiftX #2
>>
>> _rlnMicrographShiftY #3
>>
>>    1   21.31736   19.40511
>>
>>    2   20.576.............. etc........ (until 50 frames)
>>
>> I will not bore you with the different things I have tried, but using the 
>> first .star file or trying to use the different .star files from the motion 
>> folder just spits out errors. I have tried importing them into relion as 
>> well, but this does not change anything. I have the feeling the discrepancy 
>> between what the manual says I should have for a polishing input and what I 
>> have been given in the motion folder may be the source of the problem. But 
>> I am unsure how to verify this or how to solve it.
>>
>> If anyone could shed some light on this that would be much appreciated.
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Dylan Noone
>>
>
